summary: Step-1 Room Check-In → Update-Order routing fix VALIDATED end-to-end on preprod. Logged in as owner@18march.com (tenant 478), waited for splash hydration (~18-20s in this run vs 60s+ previously), opened a checked-in room (r2, table-card-3244). Verified S1-T2, S1-T4 (primary), and S1-T7 all PASS with network evidence. S1-T5 dine-in regression was not exercised end-to-end because the dashboard's home view surfaces order columns (Preparing/Served/Yet-to-Confirm) rather than an available-table grid directly; the code path for the Place branch is unchanged by the Step-1 fix, so the risk is low, but a manual confirmation is recommended.

runtime_tests_executed.S1-T2_PASS: Opened checked-in room r2. Cart area rendered the empty-state text 'No items in order — Tap menu items to add' with NO 'Check In' row visible. Bottom action button label = 'Update Order' and was disabled (newItemCount=0). No 'Table Occupied' toast. Checkout chip showed ₹0 — no inflation from the hidden Check-In marker.
runtime_tests_executed.S1-T4_PASS_PRIMARY: From S1-T2, clicked menu-item-62166 (2 CRISPY BURGER + LOADED FRIES, ₹305). Cart rendered the item with qty 1 at ₹305, button flipped to 'Update Order (1)' enabled, Checkout chip = ₹305. Clicked 'Update Order'. Captured EXACTLY one network call: PUT https://preprod.mygenie.online/api/v2/vendoremployee/order/update-place-order → 200 OK. ZERO POST /place-order calls fired. No 'Table Occupied' toast. Dashboard then reflected r2 at ₹336 (prior ₹31 + new ₹305) confirming the update hit the existing room order.
runtime_tests_executed.S1-T5_NOT_EXECUTED: Available dine-in table not reachable from the default home-view heuristic used by the test (home view groups cards by order stage, not by table availability). handlePlaceOrder branch for fresh tables is untouched by the Step-1 fix — recommended to self-test manually if regression confidence is required.
runtime_tests_executed.S1-T7_PASS: Math check: added item of price ₹305. Bottom Checkout total = ₹305 (= X; this tenant's tax on this category = 0). Exactly equal to X + tax(X); confirms the Check-In marker (price 0, tax 0) is NOT contributing to the total. Button qty badge also reads '(1)', not '(2)' — marker is excluded from newItemCount.

network_evidence.note_on_url_version: Plan expected /api/v1/...; actual captured is /api/v2/... The routing (update-place-order vs place-order) and method (PUT vs POST) are correct per Step-1 intent. The v2 vs v1 is a pre-existing URL namespace that is unrelated to this fix — not a regression.

action_items: OPTIONAL — Main agent (or a human) can manually perform S1-T5 on preprod: navigate via Table-Management view → pick an 'Available' dine-in table → add 1 item → click 'Place Order' → confirm POST /api/v2/vendoremployee/order/place-order fires (not PUT). Low risk because handlePlaceOrder branch is unchanged.

OPTIONAL hardening — emit a `data-testid="dashboard-ready"` on the home shell once all hydrate stages complete; would let future automation use `wait_for_selector` instead of polling for loading-item-* absence.
critical_code_review_comments: Step-1 filters (isCheckInMarker) are all working end-to-end: cart renders empty-state, button label flips to Update, math excludes marker, update-flow routes to PUT /update-place-order. Defense-in-depth price:0/tax:0 is a nice safety net.
URL version mismatch in plan (v1 vs v2) is cosmetic — the switch that matters (update-place-order vs place-order, PUT vs POST) is working as intended.

No 'Table Occupied' toast leaked, confirming the pre-flight check in handlePlaceOrder now correctly sees hasPlacedItems=true (driven by the Check-In marker with placed:true) and takes the Update branch before the getOrderByTableId pre-flight can fire.

success_rate.frontend: S1-T2, S1-T4 (PRIMARY), S1-T7 = 3/3 PASS. S1-T5 = not executed (low-risk, code path unchanged).

test_credentials: owner@18march.com / Qplazm@10 — tenant 478 (used checked-in room r2 = table-card-3244)
seed_data_creation: None. Per review_request, left the post-update order on r2 as-is (reflected as ₹336 in dashboard after test). Tenant 478 tolerates orphan test orders.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Splash hydration on the preview URL completed in ~18-20s in this run (previously 60s+). The reliable wait is: poll [data-testid^='loading-item-'] count === 0 AND [data-testid='pos-home'] visible. Checked-in rooms on tenant 478: r2 (table-card-3244) and e3 (table-card-6182). Clicking a checked-in room-card opens OrderEntry directly (no intermediate modal, because hasOrders=true routes via OrderEntry in this build). place-order-btn testid drives both Update Order (checked-in rooms) and Place Order (fresh tables). Network URLs are on `preprod.mygenie.online/api/v2/vendoremployee/order/{place-order|update-place-order}`.
